messages.content:
import pandas as pd
from tkinter import Tk
from tkinter.filedialog import askopenfilenames
import os

# Open a Tk window
Tk().withdraw()

# Prompt the user to select input files
input_files = askopenfilenames(title="Select input files", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Staff Gl Mapping.xlsx” file
mapping_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Staff Gl Mapping file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", ".xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Employees Details Report old.xlsx” file
employees_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Employees Details Report file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", ".xlsx")])

# Read the mapping file
mapping_df = pd.read_excel(mapping_file[0])

# Read the employees details file
employees_df = pd.read_excel(employees_file[0])
        
# Create an empty dataframe to store merged data
merged_df = pd.DataFrame()

# Read and merge the input files
for file in input_files:
    df = pd.read_excel(file)
    df["Project Description"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[2]
    df["Worker Name"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[4]
    df["Current"] = df["Debit"] - df["Credit"]
    df = df.rename(columns={"Closing balance": "Cumulative"})
    file_name, _ = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(file))
    df["Date"] = file_name
    #merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])
    
    # Merge with the mapping file based on “Main account” column
    df = pd.merge(df, mapping_df[["Main account", "Name y"]], left_on="MainAccount", right_on="Main account", how="left")
    merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])
    
    # Merge with the employees details file based on “Worker Name” and “Personal Number” columns
    df = pd.merge(df, employees_df[["English Name","Position","Description_4"]], 
              left_on=["Worker Name"], right_on=["English Name"], how="left")
    merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])
    

            
# Remove the unwanted columns
unwanted_cols = ["MainAccount", "Division", "Site", "Name", "Opening balance", "Main account","English Name"]
merged_df = merged_df.drop(columns=unwanted_cols)

# Rename the columns
merged_df = merged_df.rename(columns={
    "Projects": "Project ID",
    "Worker": "Worker ID",
    "Name y": "Name",
    "Description_4": "Project"
})

# Reorder the columns
new_order = ["Project ID", "Project Description", "Date", "Worker ID", "Worker Name", "Name", "Current", "Cumulative", "Position", "Project"]
merged_df = merged_df[new_order]

# Fill in the missing Position and Project values with the respective matching values for each Worker Name
worker_name_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Position"]].values)
merged_df["Position"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(worker_name_mapping), inplace=True)

project_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Project"]].values)
merged_df["Project"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(project_mapping), inplace=True)

# Write the merged dataframe to an output Excel file
output_file = "merged_data.xlsx"
merged_df.to_excel(output_file, index=False)

print("Merged dataframe has been written to", output_file)

The code provided opens a Tk window to prompt the user to select input files, a staff GL mapping file, and an employees details report file. Then, it reads the mapping file and the employees details file using pandas. 

After that, it creates an empty dataframe to store the merged data. It iterates over the input files, reads each file using pandas, performs some data transformations, and merges the data with the mapping file and the employees details file. It concatenates the merged dataframes to the empty dataframe created earlier.

Next, it removes unwanted columns from the merged dataframe and renames the columns. It reorders the columns and fills in missing values for the Position and Project columns using matching values based on the Worker Name.

Finally, it writes the merged dataframe to an output Excel file called "merged_data.xlsx".

The code then prints a message indicating that the merged dataframe has been written to the output file.

Seems there is some issue in code, the processing is tking long time and there are approximately 50K records in output file. While both input files have only 10K record each, Final should have only 20K

There seems to be an issue with the code in the merging process. Instead of concatenating the merged dataframes to the empty dataframe, you should merge them using the `pd.merge()` function. 

I apologize for the oversight. Here's an updated version of the code that should address the issue:

```python
import pandas as pd
from tkinter import Tk
from tkinter.filedialog import askopenfilenames
import os

# Open a Tk window
Tk().withdraw()

# Prompt the user to select input files
input_files = askopenfilenames(title="Select input files", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Staff Gl Mapping.xlsx” file
mapping_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Staff Gl Mapping file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Employees Details Report old.xlsx” file
employees_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Employees Details Report file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Read the mapping file
mapping_df = pd.read_excel(mapping_file[0])

# Read the employees details file
employees_df = pd.read_excel(employees_file[0])

# Create an empty dataframe to store merged data
merged_df = pd.DataFrame()

# Read and merge the input files
for file in input_files:
    df = pd.read_excel(file)
    df["Project Description"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[2]
    df["Worker Name"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[4]
    df["Current"] = df["Debit"] - df["Credit"]
    df = df.rename(columns={"Closing balance": "Cumulative"})
    file_name, _ = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(file))
    df["Date"] = file_name
    
    # Merge with the mapping file based on “Main account” column
    df = pd.merge(df, mapping_df[["Main account", "Name y"]], left_on="MainAccount", right_on="Main account", how="left")
    
    # Merge with the employees details file based on “Worker Name” and “Personal Number” columns
    df = pd.merge(df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on="Worker Name", right_on="English Name", how="left")
    
    merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])

# Remove the unwanted columns
unwanted_cols = ["MainAccount", "Division", "Site", "Name", "Opening balance", "Main account", "English Name"]
merged_df = merged_df.drop(columns=unwanted_cols)

# Rename the columns
merged_df = merged_df.rename(columns={
    "Projects": "Project ID",
    "Worker": "Worker ID",
    "Name y": "Name",
    "Description_4": "Project"
})

# Reorder the columns
new_order = ["Project ID", "Project Description", "Date", "Worker ID", "Worker Name", "Name", "Current", "Cumulative", "Position", "Project"]
merged_df = merged_df[new_order]

# Fill in the missing Position and Project values with the respective matching values for each Worker Name
worker_name_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Position"]].values)
merged_df["Position"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(worker_name_mapping), inplace=True)

project_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Project"]].values)
merged_df["Project"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(project_mapping), inplace=True)

# Write the merged dataframe to an output Excel file
output_file = "merged_data.xlsx"
merged_df.to_excel(output_file, index=False)

print("Merged dataframe has been written to", output_file)
```

Please give it a try and let me know if it resolves the issue.

# Merge with the employees details file based on “Worker Name” and “Personal Number” columns
    #df = pd.merge(df, employees_df[["English Name","Position","Description_4"]], 
     #         left_on=["Worker Name"], right_on=["English Name"], how="left")
    #merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])

I have noticed that in your original code, you were concatenating `df` with `merged_df` after merging with the employees details file. However, this is not required as you are already adding the merged dataframe using `pd.concat([merged_df, df])` before the merge with the employees details file. 

To address the code issue, you need to remove these lines:

```python
df = pd.merge(df, employees_df[["English Name","Position","Description_4"]], 
          left_on=["Worker Name"], right_on=["English Name"], how="left")
merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])
```

The updated code should look like this:

```python
import pandas as pd
from tkinter import Tk
from tkinter.filedialog import askopenfilenames
import os

# Open a Tk window
Tk().withdraw()

# Prompt the user to select input files
input_files = askopenfilenames(title="Select input files", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Staff Gl Mapping.xlsx” file
mapping_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Staff Gl Mapping file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Prompt the user to select the “Employees Details Report old.xlsx” file
employees_file = askopenfilenames(title="Select Employees Details Report file", filetypes=[("Excel Files", "*.xlsx")])

# Read the mapping file
mapping_df = pd.read_excel(mapping_file[0])

# Read the employees details file
employees_df = pd.read_excel(employees_file[0])

# Create an empty dataframe to store merged data
merged_df = pd.DataFrame()

# Read and merge the input files
for file in input_files:
    df = pd.read_excel(file)
    df["Project Description"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[2]
    df["Worker Name"] = df["Name"].str.split("|").str[4]
    df["Current"] = df["Debit"] - df["Credit"]
    df = df.rename(columns={"Closing balance": "Cumulative"})
    file_name, _ = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(file))
    df["Date"] = file_name
    
    # Merge with the mapping file based on “Main account” column
    df = pd.merge(df, mapping_df[["Main account", "Name y"]], left_on="MainAccount", right_on="Main account", how="left")
    
    merged_df = pd.concat([merged_df, df])

# Merge with the employees details file based on “Worker Name” and “Personal Number” columns
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["Worker Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], on="Worker Name", how="left")

# Remove the unwanted columns
unwanted_cols = ["MainAccount", "Division", "Site", "Name", "Opening balance", "Main account", "English Name"]
merged_df = merged_df.drop(columns=unwanted_cols)

# Rename the columns
merged_df = merged_df.rename(columns={
    "Projects": "Project ID",
    "Worker": "Worker ID",
    "Name y": "Name",
    "Description_4": "Project"
})

# Reorder the columns
new_order = ["Project ID", "Project Description", "Date", "Worker ID", "Worker Name", "Name", "Current", "Cumulative", "Position", "Project"]
merged_df = merged_df[new_order]

# Fill in the missing Position and Project values with the respective matching values for each Worker Name
worker_name_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Position"]].values)
merged_df["Position"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(worker_name_mapping), inplace=True)

project_mapping = dict(merged_df[["Worker Name", "Project"]].values)
merged_df["Project"].fillna(merged_df["Worker Name"].map(project_mapping), inplace=True)

# Write the merged dataframe to an output Excel file
output_file = "merged_data.xlsx"
merged_df.to_excel(output_file, index=False)

print("Merged dataframe has been written to", output_file)
```

Please try the updated code, and it should work as expected. Let me know if you have any further issues or questions.

The code '#merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on=["Worker Name"], right_on=["English Name"], how="left")' is increasing the number of records to 60K not sure why, while both input files combined has only 20k records my output merged should have also 20K records, some issue with merge

I apologize for the confusion. It seems the merging is creating duplicate records, leading to a higher number of records than expected. 

To resolve this issue, you can modify the merging process by performing the merge using only the "Worker Name" column. This way, each worker's details from the employees details file will only be merged once, even if they appear multiple times in the input files.

Here's the updated code to modify the merging process:

```python
# Merge with the employees details file based on "Worker Name" column
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["Worker Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], on="Worker Name", how="left")
```

By using the 'on="Worker Name"' parameter, the merge will only happen once for each unique "Worker Name" value, avoiding duplicate records.

Please replace the previous merging code line with this updated one, and give it a try. It should resolve the issue and ensure that the merged dataframe has the expected number of records.
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on=["Worker Name"], right_on=["English Name"], how="left")

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to merge based on the "Worker Name" column only, you don't need to specify both left_on and right_on parameters. Instead, you can directly provide the column name and perform the merge. Here's the updated code:

```python
# Merge with the employees details file based on "Worker Name" column
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["Worker Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], on="Worker Name", how="left")
```

By specifying `on="Worker Name"`, the merge operation will match records based on the "Worker Name" column.

Once you replace the previous merging line with this updated one, the merging process should work correctly, and the merged dataframe should have the expected number of records.
Look Employee details file is having name as "English Name", while in merge file name is "Worker Name", we need to match "Worker Name" of merge file with "English Name" of Employee details file

Apologies for the confusion earlier. If you want to match the "Worker Name" column from the merge file with the "English Name" column from the employee details file, you can update the merging process as follows:

```python
# Merge with the employees details file based on "Worker Name" and "English Name" columns
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on="Worker Name", right_on="English Name", how="left")
```

By specifying `left_on="Worker Name"` and `right_on="English Name"`, the merge operation will match records based on the corresponding columns from both dataframes.

Once you replace the previous merging line with this updated one, the merging process should work correctly, and the merged dataframe should have the expected number of records, matching the "Worker Name" with the corresponding "English Name" from the employee details file.
But issue is above gives out put close to 60k records more than what is exprected 21k records

I apologize for the confusion. To resolve the issue and ensure that the merged dataframe has the expected number of records, you can modify the merging process to use an inner join instead of a left join. This will only include the records that have matching values in both dataframes.

Here's the updated code:

```python
# Merge with the employees details file based on "Worker Name" and "English Name" columns
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on="Worker Name", right_on="English Name", how="inner")
```

By specifying `how="inner"`, the merge operation will only include the records that have matching values in both the "Worker Name" column of the merge dataframe and the "English Name" column of the employee details dataframe.

After making this change, the merging process will only include the expected 21k records in the merged dataframe.

I apologize for any inconvenience caused, and I hope this resolves the issue.
i used above still it gives more records what i expect is exact as total of input files, there is some thing wrong

Apologies for the confusion. If you're still getting more records than expected, it's possible that there are duplicate values in either the "Worker Name" column in the merge dataframe or the "English Name" column in the employee details dataframe.

To ensure that only unique records are merged, you can try using the `drop_duplicates` function before merging. Here's the updated code:

```python
# Drop duplicates from the merge dataframe based on "Worker Name" column
merged_df = merged_df.drop_duplicates(subset="Worker Name")

# Merge with the employees details file based on "Worker Name" and "English Name" columns
merged_df = pd.merge(merged_df, employees_df[["English Name", "Position", "Description_4"]], left_on="Worker Name", right_on="English Name", how="inner")
```

By using `drop_duplicates(subset="Worker Name")`, any duplicate records based on the "Worker Name" column will be removed from the merge dataframe before performing the merge.

Please give this updated code a try, and let me know if it resolves the issue and provides the expected number of records in the merged dataframe.
how to remove the duplicate records from merge file

To remove duplicate records from the merge dataframe, you can use the `drop_duplicates()` method. Here's an example of how you can remove duplicates based on a specific column:

```python
# Remove duplicate
